title: JavaScript Scope & Variables
description: JavaScript variables can be declared using let, const, and var. Understanding their lexical scope, hoisting, and re-assignment characteristics is critical for clean code.
details: Unlike 'var' which is function-scoped and hoisted to the top of its context, 'let' and 'const' are block-scoped and exist within the Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) until their declaration is evaluated.
